claims.title: Communication Failure — Kayani to Kamath
claims.defendant: Dr. Imran Kayani / Florida Medical Clinic

counters.label: Independent Duty / Shared EMR Severs Causation
counters.summary: Dr. Kamath had independent duty to review the chart. Shared EMR access may satisfy disclosure obligation. Intervening negligence doctrine points to Kamath.
counters.ourRebuttal: Non-delegable duty under Fla. Stat. §766.102. Kayani initiated referral; patient is a layperson who cannot assess RFA risk.

counters.label: No Peer Standard Mandates Cross-Provider Disclosure for RFA
counters.summary: Defense expert will testify U.S. Army CPG is military/specialty guidance, not civilian PCP standard. No universal requirement to call the proceduralist.
counters.legalBasis: Standard of care — clinical judgment defense
counters.ourRebuttal: Published RFA/rhabdo literature + same-system referral created heightened duty.

counters.label: Patient's Own Disclosure Obligation
counters.summary: Kevin knew about his 2024 hospitalization. Florida comparative fault: jury can reduce damages if Kevin didn't tell Kamath himself.
counters.ourRebuttal: Layperson cannot assess procedural risk. Non-delegable physician duty.

counters.label: Same-System Referral Standard — No Verbal Disclosure Required
counters.summary: Within FMC multi-specialty group, shared record is the standard. No obligation for a separate verbal or written disclosure.
counters.ourRebuttal: Shared EMR doesn't mean Kamath actually reviewed it. No evidence he did.

claims.title: 14-Year Diagnostic Failure — CK Never Ordered
claims.defendant: Dr. Imran Kayani / Florida Medical Clinic

counters.label: Mayo Full Genome Negative — No Diagnosable Condition Existed
counters.summary: Mayo Clinic ran full genome sequencing. No genetic findings related to rhabdomyolysis. VUS in AGK/AMPD1 are not pathogenic. Defense calls Mayo geneticist: no underlying condition to diagnose.
counters.legalBasis: No duty without foreseeable harm; causation failure
counters.ourRebuttal: Clinical diagnosis doesn't require genetic confirmation. Two rhabdo episodes = clinical pattern. Dr. Al review may identify what Mayo missed.

counters.label: CK Omission Was Clinically Reasonable — Retrospective Bias
counters.summary: Fatigue and myalgia are ubiquitous. PCP standard doesn't require CK on every such patient. Defense expert testifies omission was within standard of care.
counters.legalBasis: Clinical judgment defense; hindsight bias argument
counters.ourRebuttal: Pattern of symptoms + nephrolithiasis + recurrent myalgia = specific indicators, not generic complaints.

counters.label: Causation Gap — Positive CK Wouldn't Have Prevented the Events
counters.summary: Even if CK was drawn Jan 19, the 6-day timeline is too compressed to prevent Jan 25 hospitalization. Same for Jul 29 / Aug 6 sequence.
counters.ourRebuttal: Earlier detection = metabolic referral = protocols in place = RFA performed differently or not at all.

counters.label: Nephrolithiasis Is Not a CK Trigger in General Practice
counters.summary: Defense nephrologist/urologist testifies kidney stones are managed as their own condition, not a metabolic myopathy screening trigger.
counters.legalBasis: Standard of care — specialty guideline vs. PCP practice
counters.ourRebuttal: Combination of symptoms (fatigue + myalgia + nephrolithiasis + exercise intolerance) together triggers MMS screening guidelines.

claims.title: Post-Crisis Monitoring Failure
claims.defendant: Dr. Imran Kayani / Florida Medical Clinic

counters.label: Reasonable Reliance on Specialist Continuity of Care
counters.summary: With rheumatology, neurology, and pain management active, PCP is entitled to rely on specialists for specialized monitoring. Multi-specialist care reduces PCP's individual monitoring duty.
counters.legalBasis: Division of labor in multi-specialty care; specialist reliance doctrine
counters.ourRebuttal: Kayani never transferred monitoring responsibility. No documented handoff. He remained PCP of record.

counters.label: Aug 2025 Event Not Caused by Monitoring Gap
counters.summary: RFA was the trigger, not the absence of CK monitoring. Even with regular CK draws, the July 18 RFA still happens without communication of the 2024 rhabdo.
counters.ourRebuttal: Proper monitoring triggers metabolic referral which triggers pre-RFA CK screening and risk assessment.

counters.label: Rheumatology Directive Was Advisory, Not a Binding Order
counters.summary: Consult recommendations are advisory. Kayani retained clinical discretion to determine pace and sequence of follow-up. Patient was clinically stable.
counters.legalBasis: Clinical autonomy; advisory vs. binding directives
counters.ourRebuttal: PCP of record has non-delegable duty to implement specialist recommendations or document clinical rationale for deviation.

counters.label: Patient Failed to Advocate for Testing
counters.summary: Kevin could have requested CK testing at any September 2025 visit. Comparative fault for failure to advocate for own monitoring.
counters.ourRebuttal: Physician has superior knowledge. Patient relies on physician to implement appropriate monitoring protocols.

counters.label: Statutory Liability Falls on the Insurer, Not the Physician
counters.summary: Fla. Stat. §627.42392 electronic submission mandate governs insurers processing PAs. Rosario's office submitted; insurer controlled processing. Delay may be FEPBLUE's statutory violation, not Rosario's.
counters.ourRebuttal: Staff instructed Kevin to 'hold off' — delay began at submission stage, not processing. Paper trail shows non-submission, not insurer delay.

counters.label: VUS Results Are Non-Actionable — No Physical Harm from Delay
counters.summary: Results returned two VUS — not pathogenic. VUS don't change treatment plans or support definitive accommodation requests. Earlier results = same outcome.
counters.legalBasis: Causation — no harm without actionable result
counters.ourRebuttal: Economic harm from RIF/accommodations timing is separate from treatment plan. Even VUS documentation supports workplace accommodation under ADA/Rehabilitation Act.

counters.label: RIF Employment Damages Are Too Speculative to Recover
counters.summary: Causation chain (delay → late results → no accommodation → RIF harm) is entirely speculative. Summary judgment motion likely on this damages theory.
counters.ourRebuttal: Timeline correlation + documented patient contacts + staff 'hold off' instruction creates sufficient factual dispute for jury.

counters.label: 'Hold Off' Instruction Is a Credibility Dispute
counters.summary: Kevin's recollection of a phone call vs. staff records. Without recording or written evidence, this is he-said-she-said. Juries discount uncorroborated patient testimony.
counters.ourRebuttal: Portal messages and call timeline corroborate pattern. Multiple contacts + 48-day gap speaks for itself.

counters.label: No Objective Evidence of Permanent Injury
counters.summary: EMG normal. MRI/MRA normal. Mayo genome negative. CK normalized. Autoimmune panels negative. Dr. Pantol said 'probable,' not confirmed. Florida requires proof of permanent injury to reasonable medical certainty.
counters.legalBasis: Permanent injury standard; reasonable medical certainty
counters.ourRebuttal: Muscle biopsy and functional testing at Mayo pending. Clinical confirmation from multiple treating providers. Functional impairment documented by patient.

counters.label: Pre-Existing Condition / Inevitable Harm
counters.summary: If Kevin has recurrent idiopathic rhabdomyolysis (unknown cause), another episode was inevitable regardless of these doctors' actions. Harm was not caused by negligence — it was caused by the underlying condition.
counters.ourRebuttal: Predisposition ≠ inevitable severity. Manageable with proper protocols. The negligence converted manageable into catastrophic.

counters.label: Economic Damages Are Speculative — No Expert Foundation
counters.summary: $650K–$1.3M estimate is Kevin's own calculation. No forensic economist, no vocational expert, no actuarial FERS analysis. Defense Daubert challenge will exclude ungrounded figures.
counters.ourRebuttal: Retain forensic economist and vocational expert as part of litigation prep. Figures are conservative based on documented earnings and career trajectory.

counters.label: Failure to Mitigate — Kevin Is Working
counters.summary: Kevin returned to duty November 13, 2025. If earning capacity is impaired, he must prove it — not just assert future risk. Employment undermines catastrophic impairment narrative.
counters.ourRebuttal: Working ≠ fully recovered. Reduced capacity, accommodation needs, and elevated disability risk are present even while employed.

overarchingDefenses.label: Perfect Storm / Coincidence Theory
overarchingDefenses.summary: RFA-induced rhabdo is rare. 18-day gap is circumstantial. Informed consent for RFA likely listed rhabdo as a risk.

overarchingDefenses.label: Mayo Expert Dominates the Expert Battle
overarchingDefenses.summary: Defense has full-genome sequencing from Mayo Clinic (negative). Kevin has 'probable' diagnoses from treating physicians without diagnostic confirmation.

overarchingDefenses.label: No Confirmed Diagnosis = No Manageable Condition = No Negligence
overarchingDefenses.summary: Core theory requires confirmed underlying condition. Without it, defense files summary judgment motion.
